stories.goal: configure Ollama as my LLM provider during iSDLC installation
stories.benefit: I can use free local models without paying for Anthropic API access

acceptance_criteria.given: I am running the iSDLC installer (installer.js, install.sh, or install.ps1)
acceptance_criteria.when: the installer reaches the provider selection step
acceptance_criteria.then: I see a choice between [1] Claude Code (Anthropic API) and [2] Ollama (local/free models)
my provider preference is recorded in the project configuration (providers.yaml or equivalent)

acceptance_criteria.when: the installer displays the final 'Next Steps' section
acceptance_criteria.then: I see instructions to install Ollama and recommended models (qwen3-coder, glm-4.7, gpt-oss) followed by 'Launch claude in your project directory'

acceptance_criteria.given: I selected Anthropic API during installation
acceptance_criteria.when: the installer displays the final 'Next Steps' section
acceptance_criteria.then: I see instructions to set up my Anthropic API key followed by 'Launch claude in your project directory'

acceptance_criteria.when: regardless of which provider I selected
acceptance_criteria.then: the installer does NOT attempt to install Ollama or download models

stories.goal: have the framework auto-detect my LLM provider when I launch Claude Code
stories.benefit: I don't need to manually configure environment variables

acceptance_criteria.given: I have Ollama running locally and selected Ollama during install
acceptance_criteria.when: I launch claude in my project directory
acceptance_criteria.then: the framework auto-detects Ollama and routes LLM requests to it without manual env var setup

acceptance_criteria.given: I have an Anthropic API key configured
acceptance_criteria.when: I launch claude in my project directory
acceptance_criteria.then: the framework uses Anthropic API as before (no regression)

acceptance_criteria.given: Ollama is not running and no Anthropic API key is set
acceptance_criteria.then: I see a clear error message suggesting how to configure a provider (not an unhandled exception)

stories.goal: manage my LLM provider configuration using the /provider command
stories.benefit: I can switch between providers, check status, and troubleshoot configuration

acceptance_criteria.then: the provider management interface loads (command is not disabled)
I see Ollama listed as available with its running models

stories.goal: know which models are recommended for iSDLC and their requirements
stories.benefit: I choose models that will work well with the framework's multi-agent workflows

acceptance_criteria.then: I see current recommended models (qwen3-coder, glm-4.7, gpt-oss) with context window sizes and VRAM requirements

acceptance_criteria.given: I read the CLAUDE.md in my project
acceptance_criteria.then: I see quick-start instructions, recommended models, minimum context window (64k), and known limitations
I see documented risks: complex multi-agent workflows may have reduced quality, tool calling varies by model, structured output reliability varies
